title: veronica rotunda subsp. subintegra
intro: Veronica rotunda subsp. subintegra, also known as the Round-leaved Speedwell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Veronica rotunda subsp. subintegra include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
